sizing_rule.note: Fractional f method: Use 20% (0.2) of optimal f (1.01933 geometric mean) instead of full optimal f. Fractional geometric mean FG = 1.005. Solve for contract count N using: 1.005^N <= 1.01933^(N*0.2) + 1 - 0.2. This is Kelly criterion with 0.2 fractional scaling to reduce variance while maintaining mathematical optimization.

source_rule_text: Position sizing using fractional f method where active equity percentage (FRAC) determines the fraction of optimal f to use, with geometric mean calculations to find the appropriate number of contracts (N).
